app-remote.description: IPC granted to the live web app over HTTPS. The remote page is treated as semi-trusted (assume MITM/compromise risk), so it is granted ONLY the non-privileged runtime-info command, the sign-video-challenge attestation command, and the core baseline needed for IPC. sign-video-challenge is a DELIBERATE remote grant: it returns only an HMAC signature over a server-issued nonce (single-use, server-verified), so the page can never exfiltrate the build-time secret or mint reusable credentials. The privileged commands (OS keyring secrets, offline cache, speaking-audio temp files, dropped-file probing, notifications, open-external) are deliberately NOT granted to the remote origin — they stay registered for localhost/dev and future native re-enablement. Re-add the matching allow-* here only if a feature is moved to native desktop capture.

dev-localhost.description: Dev-only: the same minimal IPC surface as app-remote (runtime-info plus the signature-only, server-nonce-bound sign-video-challenge attestation command — the secret itself can never be exfiltrated), granted to the local Next.js dev server (http://localhost:3000) that `tauri dev` loads. The navigation guard in lib.rs only permits loopback origins in dev builds, so this never applies in a packaged app even though the capability is compiled in.
